title: National Maritime Day: India's oil lifeline churns through conflict waters
description: On most days, India's economic heartbeat is invisible. It does not pulse through stock exchanges or policy corridors but across vast, restless waters where ships move quietly between continents. Yet, when conflict erupts in narrow choke points like the Strait of Hormuz, that invisible system becomes the country’s most visible vulnerability. The ongoing tensions have done exactly that, forcing India to confront a reality it has long managed but rarely foregrounded.

This decision comes as France faces increasing defense pressures and competing priorities, highlighting Paris's commitment to maintaining sovereign control over its Rafale program.

‘Drugged, raped, forced into marriage': Hyderabad woman alleges coerced conversion
description: she provided an outline of a harrowing experience involving deception, physical violence, financial exploitation, and religious coercion. The victim met the accused, Nawaz, when he approached her to rent a shop. She alleged he falsely claimed to have converted to Hinduism and changed his name to Navadurga to gain her trust.

title: Raghav Chadha made to stand like rooster, beaten in ‘secret mansion’: Ex-AAP leader
description: A political row has erupted within the party following allegations against Raghav Chadha. Chadha has vehemently denied these accusations, labeling them a coordinated campaign. This public exchange of claims signifies an escalating internal divide within the party.

title: 'Goodwill’ lift turned fatal: Engineering students kill drunk driver, flee with car
description: Three engineering students, driven by greed for a red Alto car, murdered an intoxicated man they offered to help. After initially assisting Suchit Bhojapure, they decided to steal his vehicle and possessions, leading to his brutal killing and disposal of his body. The students were apprehended after blood stains on their shoes betrayed their lies.

title: Packers linked to $6M ex-Commanders veteran after strong season performance
description: The Green Bay Packers recorded a pretty average performance in the last season with 9 wins and 8 losses, sparking stress among fans. While the team’s performance declined since the 2024 season, when they had recorded 11 wins and just 6 losses, it seems the Green Bay Packers are fully focused on fixing their roster and could be eyeing a veteran.

title: 12-year-old boy exposes mother, sister's plot to kill father in Uttarakhand
description: A woman and her daughter have been arrested for allegedly murdering the husband and father in Haridwar. The 12-year-old son's testimony exposed the crime, revealing the mother and daughter allegedly conspired to kill the victim due to his opposition to their separate relationships. The post-mortem confirmed strangulation.

title: Meta CTO’s message to engineers and college graduates looking to join technology industry
description: Meta's CTO, Andrew Bosworth, urged aspiring tech professionals to prioritize hands-on building over theoretical study. He emphasized immersing oneself in practical projects, from "vibe coding" with AI tools for software to using Raspberry Pi and Arduino for hardware.  Bosworth advocates for continuous experimentation and action to develop essential skills for the evolving tech landscape.

title: Amid job cuts, Canadian billionaire Kevin O’Leary points out a good thing about AI layoffs
description: Kevin O'Leary, the Canadian billionaire, asserts that artificial intelligence is elevating human creativity, making artists and storytellers more valuable than ever. He highlights how compelling social media content, especially short-form videos, now commands significant salaries due to measurable customer acquisition. O'Leary believes AI is a powerful productivity tool, fostering new industries and opportunities across all economic sectors.

title: Candace Owens claims win as Blake Lively case vs Justin Baldoni dismissed
description: Candace Owens reacted after a federal judge dismissed most of Blake Lively’s claims against Justin Baldoni, leaving only three issues for trial. The ruling removed serious allegations like sexual harassment and defamation. The case will now focus on retaliation claims when the trial begins on May 18, 2026, as reported by The Guardian and Entertainment Weekly.
